steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content:
Most studies worldwide—North American and international (like Luo's in China)—found the same positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury, while only Porvari and Verta's study produced a different result.

This overwhelming agreement across regions demonstrates the association is real and widespread, not region-specific.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
”Dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels do typically rise and fall together in fresh water” directly reflects the evidence: most studies worldwide found the association, so “typically” captures the general pattern with rare exceptions.

B contradicts this by claiming the association is particular to North America, but Luo's study in China demonstrates the same positive association exists outside North America.

A and D address unrelated topics: absolute concentration levels and alleged measurement errors.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
